shortcomings: Missing explicit mention of prompt templates, system instructions, and guardrails—the job emphasizes designing prompts and guardrails, but the resume only notes general policy checks and human-in-the-loop validation in one project.
No explicit reference to automated regression tests or a formal LLM evaluation framework beyond the RAG evaluation suite, which could be seen as insufficient for enterprise-grade QA.
Lacks a clear, explicit description of multi-step workflows and multi-agent architectures; projects hint at agentic systems but do not clearly articulate multi-step or multi-agent flow design.

Retrieval strategies are not fully detailed; there is a hybrid retrieval engine example (MongoDB, S3) but little on retrieval strategies, document/document retrieval pipelines, or ranking/selection methods.
Embeddings experience is not explicitly demonstrated; the resume mentions embedding architectures in an ablation study but does not show hands-on usage of embeddings or embedding tooling.
ATS/parse risk: the resume is in LaTeX source, which many ATS systems cannot parse reliably; lack of a plain-text or ATS-friendly version could reduce initial screening success.

Location/relocation signal is weak: current location is listed as San Jose, CA; job location is Santa Clara, CA; while nearby, there is no explicit note about willingness to relocate or remote/hybrid work.
Minor quality/control issues: a spelling error in a GitHub link (Oberservability) and inconsistent capitalization/typo risk in project titles; could reflect attention to detail.
Education timeline: MSc is listed as May 2026, which may require explicit note of expected graduation to avoid misinterpretation if recruiters screen for availability.
Summary/objective section is missing; no concise value proposition at the top to frame fit for GenAI Engineer role.
experience_gaps: No clearly described multi-step and multi-agent workflow architecture (beyond hints of 'agentic' systems in projects).
No explicit mention of automated LLM evaluation/regression tests beyond a RAG evaluation suite.
No explicit discussion of prompt engineering techniques (beyond general project claims).
Limited explicit demonstration of embeddings usage or tooling (beyond terminology in coursework and RAG-related projects).
weak_sections: Education section could be clearer about current/expected graduation status and how coursework translates to job fit.
Lacks a short summary/objective at the top to quickly convey value proposition and fit for GenAI Engineer role.
LaTeX resume format may hinder ATS parsing; consider providing a plain-text or Word version for ease of screening.
overall_assessment: Strong alignment with GenAI Engineer responsibilities is evident through hands-on work with RAG, LangChain, LLMs, cloud services, and agentic systems (Evaluator-Optimizer RAG; Agentic BMC Observability). However, to avoid being skipped, the candidate should explicitly address missing keywords (prompt templates, guardrails, regression tests, multi-agent/multi-step workflows, document retrieval strategies), demonstrate clear hands-on embeddings usage, fix minor formatting/typos, and provide an ATS-friendly plain-text resume plus a brief summary and explicit relocation/availability notes.
